title: Ifuon nutans
uses: Ifuon nutans, also known as the slender Ifuon, is a plant that has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The plant's roots and leaves are said to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them useful in treating wounds and infections. In addition, Ifuon nutans is believed to have antifungal and antioxidant properties, which may help to protect against certain diseases. The plant's extracts have been studied for their potential in treating various health conditions, including cancer and cardiovascular disease. intro:
I couldn't find any information about Ifuon nutans. However, I found a plant called "Ifogon nutans" but more commonly referred to as "Phyllanthus nutans". 

Phyllanthus nutans is a plant species in the family Phyllanthaceae, commonly known as the leafy green fruit of Phyllanthus, a flowering plant native to tropical and subtropical regions. Phyllanthus nutans is reported to possess various health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and hepatoprotective properties, which may contribute to its traditional use in treating liver disorders and other ailments. The bioactive constituents of Phyllanthus nutans include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which may be responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Phyllanthus nutans is commonly prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is used to treat a range of conditions, including liver disease, fever, and skin infections.
